leagueBriefing: Olympique Marseille continued their pursuit of a Champions League spot with a hard-fought 1-0 victory over a resilient Auxerre side. In a match defined by defensive organization and missed opportunities, a clinical second-half strike from Amine Gouiri proved to be the difference, allowing Marseille to secure their third consecutive win and keep the pressure on the league leaders.

games.headline: Gouiri strike secures gritty Marseille win over Auxerre
games.brief: Olympique Marseille ground out a vital 1-0 win at the Orange Vélodrome on Friday night, overcoming a stubborn Auxerre defense that refused to buckle for much of the game. After a scoreless first half where clear-cut chances were at a premium, Amine Gouiri broke the deadlock in the 79th minute with a precise finish from the center of the box. While Auxerre fought valiantly and restricted Marseille to just three shots on target, they lacked the offensive spark to find an equalizer, ultimately succumbing to the hosts' superior individual quality.
bullets: Amine Gouiri scored the match-winner in the 79th minute, finding the bottom left corner with a clinical right-footed shot.
Marseille dominated possession with 58% and recorded 16 total shots, though only 3 were on target.
Donovan Léon kept Auxerre in the contest with several crucial interventions before finally being beaten late in the second half.
The victory marks Marseille's third straight league win, solidifying their position in the top three of Ligue 1.
Auxerre’s struggles away from home continue, as they remain precariously close to the relegation zone after 26 matches.
games.context: The result keeps Marseille firmly in the hunt for a top-two finish, while Auxerre's battle against the drop becomes increasingly urgent.
